meta.notes: Quarterly content refresh: added 20 new questions (G1-0006 to G1-0025) per content refresh schedule. IDs continue sequentially from G1-0001.

questions.question: You are approaching an intersection with a steady red traffic light and no sign prohibiting turns. What must you do before turning right on the red light?
options: Slow down and turn without stopping if no cars are coming
Come to a complete stop, check for pedestrians and cross traffic, then turn if it is safe
Sound your horn and proceed through the intersection
Wait for a green arrow before turning
questions.explanation: Unless a sign specifically prohibits it, drivers may turn right on a red light in Ontario, but only after coming to a complete stop and yielding to pedestrians and cross traffic.

questions.question: On a dry road in good conditions, how much following distance should you keep behind the vehicle ahead of you?
options: At least a 1-second gap
At least a 2-second gap
At least a 5-second gap
One car length for every 10 km/h of speed
questions.explanation: The 2-second rule is the minimum safe following distance in good conditions; it should be increased in poor weather, low visibility, or when following larger vehicles.

questions.question: As a G1 driver in Ontario, what blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit applies to you?
options: 0.08%, the same as fully licensed drivers
Zero tolerance — you must have no alcohol in your system
questions.explanation: Novice drivers in Ontario's Graduated Licensing System (G1 and G2) are subject to a zero blood alcohol concentration requirement, regardless of age.

questions.question: You are driving on a two-way road with no median. A school bus ahead has stopped with its upper alternating red lights flashing. What must you do?
options: Slow down and pass carefully
Stop, regardless of which direction you are travelling, and wait until the lights stop flashing
Only stop if you are behind the bus
Proceed if there are no children visible
questions.explanation: On roads without a median, traffic in both directions must stop for a school bus with flashing upper red lights and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing and the bus resumes moving.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a yellow, diamond-shaped road sign?
options: To give a regulatory command that must be obeyed
To warn drivers of a hazard or a change in road conditions ahead
To provide information about services such as gas or food
To mark a construction zone
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond-shaped signs are warning signs, alerting drivers to hazards or changes ahead such as curves, merges, or pedestrian crossings.

questions.question: At some Ontario intersections, what does a flashing green traffic light mean for the driver facing it?
options: You may proceed straight through or turn left while oncoming traffic is stopped on red (advance green)
It is exactly the same as a steady green light with no special meaning
Left turns are prohibited until the light stops flashing
Only pedestrians may proceed; vehicles must wait
questions.explanation: A flashing green signal is used at certain intersections to indicate an advance green: the driver facing it may go straight or turn left while opposing traffic still faces a red light.

questions.question: You arrive at a four-way stop at the same time as another vehicle approaching from your right. Who has the right-of-way?
options: You, because you must always proceed first at a four-way stop
The vehicle on your right
Whichever vehicle is larger
Whoever signals or honks first
questions.explanation: When two vehicles arrive at an uncontrolled or all-way stop intersection at the same time, the vehicle on the right has the right-of-way.

questions.question: When entering a roundabout in Ontario, who must you yield to?
options: Traffic already circulating in the roundabout
Traffic waiting to enter behind you
Only pedestrians, not other vehicles
No one — entry is on a first-come, first-served basis regardless of position
questions.explanation: Drivers entering a roundabout must yield to vehicles and cyclists already circulating within it before merging in.

questions.question: In poor weather such as heavy rain, snow, or fog, how should you adjust your following distance?
options: Keep exactly the standard 2-second gap
Increase your following distance well beyond the standard 2-second gap
Decrease your distance so you can see the road better
No adjustment is needed if your vehicle has good tires
questions.explanation: Stopping distances increase significantly in poor weather, so you should increase your following distance beyond the normal 2-second gap.

questions.question: In a designated community safety zone, what happens to fines for speeding and other traffic offences?
options: Fines are reduced to encourage compliance
Fines are doubled
Fines stay exactly the same as elsewhere
Only verbal warnings are issued, never fines
questions.explanation: Community safety zones are areas, often near schools or playgrounds, where fines for speeding and other offences are doubled to improve safety for pedestrians.

questions.question: What is hydroplaning and when is it most likely to occur?
options: Loss of tire traction with the road due to a film of water, most likely at higher speeds in heavy rain
Loss of traction that only happens on icy roads
The engine stalling after driving through deep water
A tire blowout caused by hitting a pothole
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when tires ride on a layer of water instead of the road surface, causing loss of steering and braking control; it is more likely at higher speeds and in heavy rain.

questions.question: What is an immediate consequence for a novice (G1/G2) driver caught with any amount of alcohol in their blood?
options: A verbal warning only, for a first offence
An immediate roadside licence suspension
A fine only, with no licence suspension
A deduction of 2 demerit points and nothing else
questions.explanation: Because novice drivers must maintain zero blood alcohol concentration, being caught with any alcohol in their system results in an immediate roadside licence suspension in addition to other penalties.

questions.question: How can combining alcohol with certain drugs, including some common medications, affect a driver's impairment?
options: The two substances cancel out each other's effects
There is no additional effect beyond alcohol alone
The combination can multiply impairment far beyond what either substance would cause alone
It only affects reaction time and has no effect on judgment
questions.explanation: Mixing alcohol with drugs, including many prescription and over-the-counter medications, can multiply impairing effects, making driving far more dangerous than either substance alone.

questions.question: When passing a cyclist on an Ontario road where a minimum distance is required by law, how much space must you leave?
options: At least 1 metre
No specific distance is required anywhere in Ontario
At least 3 metres in every case
Only need to slow down; no distance requirement applies
questions.explanation: Ontario law requires drivers to leave a minimum distance, at least one metre, when passing a cyclist where road and traffic conditions allow.

questions.question: A pedestrian is crossing at a marked crosswalk with no traffic signals. What must a driver do?
options: Stop and yield until the pedestrian has fully cleared the driver's lane
Only slow down and proceed carefully
Proceed once the pedestrian is halfway across
Sound the horn to signal the pedestrian to hurry
questions.explanation: Drivers must stop and remain stopped for a pedestrian in a marked crosswalk until the pedestrian has completely cleared the driver's lane of travel.

questions.question: Before opening your car door after parking on a street used by cyclists, what should you do to avoid a 'dooring' collision?
options: Open the door quickly to minimize the time it is exposed to traffic
Check your mirrors and look over your shoulder for approaching cyclists before opening the door
Nothing extra is needed as long as you are legally parked
Only the front passenger needs to check for cyclists
questions.explanation: Drivers and passengers should check mirrors and look over their shoulder for cyclists before opening a door, to avoid causing a dooring collision.

questions.question: Unless otherwise posted, how close to a fire hydrant are you permitted to park?
options: Within 1 metre
You may not park within 3 metres of a fire hydrant
Directly beside it, as long as your hazard lights are on
Within 10 centimetres, since exact spacing doesn't matter
questions.explanation: Drivers generally may not park within 3 metres of a fire hydrant, so that firefighters have clear access in an emergency.

questions.question: When parking on a hill with no curb, facing downhill, which direction should you turn your front wheels?
options: To the right, off the roadway
To the left, toward the centre line and oncoming traffic
Straight ahead, the same as on flat ground
It doesn't matter as long as the parking brake is applied
questions.explanation: With no curb, turning the front wheels to the right means that if the vehicle rolls, it moves off the road and away from traffic rather than into it.

questions.question: When merging onto a highway from an acceleration lane (on-ramp), what should you do?
options: Stop at the end of the ramp and wait for a large gap in traffic
Match the speed of highway traffic and merge smoothly when there is a safe gap
Merge immediately regardless of the traffic already on the highway
Drive slowly until another driver waves you in
questions.explanation: The acceleration lane is meant to let you build up speed to match highway traffic, so you can merge smoothly into a safe gap rather than stopping or forcing your way in.

questions.question: On a typical Ontario highway, is there a minimum speed requirement for drivers?
options: No, you may drive as slowly as you like at any time
Yes, drivers must not drive so slowly that they block the normal, reasonable movement of traffic, except when necessary for safety
The minimum speed is always exactly 80 km/h
Minimum speed rules apply only to commercial trucks
questions.explanation: Ontario law prohibits driving at a speed that unreasonably blocks or slows the normal movement of traffic, unless doing so is necessary for safety.

questions.question: If you miss your intended exit on an expressway, what should you do?
options: Stop and reverse along the shoulder back to the exit
Continue on to the next exit and find another way back
Cross the median to turn around
Pull onto the shoulder and wait for a gap to merge back to the exit
questions.explanation: You should never stop, reverse, or cross a median on an expressway. If you miss your exit, continue safely to the next exit and reroute from there.

questions.question: What shape is reserved exclusively for stop signs in Ontario, so they remain recognizable from any angle?
options: Diamond
Octagon
Triangle
Rectangle
questions.explanation: The octagon shape is used only for stop signs, allowing drivers, and even drivers approaching from behind or in poor visibility, to recognize them by shape alone.

questions.question: What colour and shape are typically used for temporary construction or work zone warning signs in Ontario?
options: Yellow diamond
Orange diamond
Blue rectangle
Green circle
questions.explanation: Orange diamond-shaped signs are used to indicate construction or maintenance zones, distinguishing them from standard yellow warning signs used for permanent hazards.

questions.question: When driving in winter conditions with snow or ice on the road, how should you adjust your driving compared to dry conditions?
options: Reduce your speed and increase your following distance well beyond the normal 2-second rule
Maintain your normal speed but simply brake a little earlier
Drive faster to maintain momentum on hills and avoid getting stuck
No adjustment is needed if your vehicle has winter tires
questions.explanation: Snow and ice greatly increase stopping distances, so drivers should reduce speed and significantly increase following distance beyond the normal 2-second rule.

questions.question: When an oncoming vehicle approaches at night, when should you dim your high-beam headlights?
options: When it is within about 150 metres
Only if the other driver flashes their lights first
Never — high beams should stay on for maximum visibility
Only when driving in fog
questions.explanation: Drivers should dim their high beams when an oncoming vehicle is within about 150 metres, to avoid blinding the other driver.
